text:
Once upon a time, in a quiet town draped in misty landscapes, there lived an owl named Oscar. Oscar wasn't your ordinary owl. He loved detective stories more than worms. That's how he knew about secrets.

One grey evening, while his steady eyes peered through ancient oaks, Oscar heard a peculiar rustle. From his lofty observatory window, he saw a shadow sneak across Mrs. Willowbank's fig tree. Strange.

He flew downstairs determined. There stood a young girl with feathers like midnight.

"Why wouldn't you want some berries?" She asked sweetly.

"I think you misunderstood."

"I don't misunderstand. I wanna help you. We could make a secret club."

Oscar listened. They plotted mysteries. He discovered hidden tunnels.

Their fun evenings ended when cats came snooping.

One dark moon there was an owlraid. They fled. Oscar's feathers felt damp.

Back came stars. They planned. Maybe a mystery book.

Their library book had skeletons.

The
